shop_address: 30100 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Чудесная церковь 14 века, построенная в готическом стиле. Ее возвели умилиаты (орден смиренников) и посвятили святому Христофору, покровителю путешественников и корабельщиков. Она двуцветная - красный кирпич и белый известняк декоративных деталей. А в 1377 году церковь стала называться Мадонна-дель-Орто (Мадонна в саду), в честь чудотворной скульптуры сидящей Мадонны с Младенцем работы Джованни Де Санти. Статуя стояла в саду скульптора и вскоре прохожие стали замечать исходящий от нее свет, появились паломники, начались исцеления. Эта новость разлетелась по всему городу, и скульптора начали уговаривать перенести Мадонну в церковь. Так в незаконченном виде она за 150 дукатов переехала на новое место. Сейчас статую можно увидеть в правой капелле от алтаря. Церковь Мадонны в саду нераздельно связана с именем Якопо Робусти, прозванного Тинторетто, который жил неподалёку и был прихожанином этой церкви. По заказу общины он написал несколько картин, в том числе  «Поклонение золотому тельцу», «Чудо св. Агнессы», «Введение во храм», «Страшный суд». Есть в храме и работы его сына Доменико «Рождество с Ангелами». Сейчас эти шедевры украшают стены церкви. Тинторетто был человеком глубоко верующим и был похоронен в церкви в 1594 году. Вблизи алтаря, в боковом приделе справа, находится его могильная плита. Здесь же в капелле с правой стороны похоронены его дети, оба художники - сын Доменико и дочь Мариетта. В 1669 году  церковь была передана цистерцианцам Ломбардии, а в 1787 - перешла в государственное управление. В капеллах боковых нефов можно увидеть картины многих выдающихся живописцев. Некогда в капелле Валье над алтарём находилась картина Джованни Беллини  «Мадонна с Младенцем» (1481). Она была украдена из церкви в 1993 году и до сих пор не найдена. Так и осталась пустовать рама картины. Вход в церковь платный 3 евро. Время посещения - февраль 2023.

shop_address: Calle al Ponte de la Guerra, 511, 30124 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
We had a wonderful stay at Hotel Ca' Del Campo with our family of four for two nights. Our room was spacious, and we were delighted to have a view of the canal right from our window. The location is fantastic, just a 5-minute walk to San Marco's, and there are plenty of excellent restaurants around.

The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful throughout our stay. We had an early flight in the morning, and they assisted us in confirming a water taxi to pick us up at 4:00 AM. They even walked with us to the deck and helped us lift our luggage onto the boat. To top it off, the staff sent us off with a bag of snacks and water since we didn't have time for breakfast.

Overall, our experience at Hotel Ca' Del Campo was exceptional, and we highly recommend it to anyone visiting the area.

Leider konnte man von 5 Synagogen nur 2 betreten, die andern waren wegen Umbau- und Renovierungsarbeiten gesperrt. Ebenso die Bäckerei. Trotzdem hat es sich für mich gelohnt, da ich davor nur wenig über die Rituale und Besonderheiten des jüdischen Lebens wusste.

Fanno da cornice al campo la casa di riposo israelitica, alcuni locali kosher, il museo ebraico, le sinagoghe tedesca e italiana oltre agli alti edifici con abitazioni private. Nel campo ci sono tre vere da pozzo quattrocentesche due in marmo rosa di Verona ed una in pietra d’Istria.
Assolutamente da vedere!!!

review_text:
Campo Marzio Roma 1933 is a hidden gem for those who appreciate fine craftsmanship. This charming boutique specializes in exquisite fountain pens and high-quality leather goods, blending Italian elegance with affordability. We first discovered it thanks to our friends, who stumbled upon it during a shopping spree. They were so impressed by the beautifully crafted passport holders that they insisted we visit—and we’re glad they did.

The selection of leather goods was stunning, with rich textures and impeccable detailing. The passport holders, in particular, stood out for their elegance and durability, and at such a reasonable price, they felt like an absolute steal. The gentleman assisting us was incredibly knowledgeable and took the time to explain the difference between cheap, mass-produced leather and the premium leather used in their products. It was a fascinating insight that deepened our appreciation for the craftsmanship.

Since purchasing mine, I’ve received countless compliments whenever I travel. It’s not just a functional piece—it’s a statement of style and quality. If you ever find yourself in Rome, Campo Marzio Roma 1933 is a must-visit for anyone who loves timeless accessories that blend beauty with practicality.

review_text: Me and my friends had the day in Venice and decided to try and walk to the Pier Luigi Penzo Stadium without using our location on our phones. After many hours we managed to get to the stadium and it didn't disappoint. Its a very unique stadium and I was very pleased with the look. There was a game on but sadly it was behind closed doors so I was able to get into the stadium. Hopefully one day I can return.

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text: Amazing place for a groundhopping experience. The stadium definitely had better times back in the days, but I liked its charm and character. The local team, Venice FC, plays in Seria B - the second Italian division. Entrance fees start from 24€ (respectively 12€ for people under 30 and above 65 years) which is quite high in my opinion. Even tough I really enjoyed the experience, beer prices are a huge rip-off. 6,5€ for 0,5l out of a plastic cup is just too much!

shop_address: Fondamenta Sant'Elena, 5, 30132 Venezia VE, Italia
review_text:
Unique stadium due to its location and arrival to it is by water, however the stadium is very basic. Three uncovered stands, three of which look as if they're constructed from meccano, with a 'main stand' that is covered however has pillars obstructing views.

Limited facilities, however my Wife said the ladies toilets are better than those at San Siro. Food kiosks sold beer, coffee,, water and soft drinks with limited food options. Beer at €5 was cheapest we had anywhere in Venice.

No program, no souvenir or fan shop or stalls by ultras or other vendors.

Ticket in curva only €12 so very cheap. Vaporetto stop short walk through pleasant green space and residential area. Not much around really. Found a little tratorria pre match to get out the rain and have a beer.

Worth a visit if you're a football or stadium hopper, however is nt the 'can't miss' yiu might have thought.

review_text: Great location. Small and welcoming. Food and beer is priced well unlike at English football games. Very Sunny in the Curva and Distinti, cold but rainproof in the Tributa Verde

review_text: Lovely little green space to relax in with some interesting and creatively designed pieces to interact with. From what I can tell the featured interactive pieces update sometimes. I wish I took more pics.

shop_address: Riva dei Sette Martiri, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Originally the site of shipyards, the gardens were transformed into public gardens in the 1930s. Located just a 10-minute walk from Piazza San Marco, the gardens offer a peaceful spot to rest and recharge away from the busier parts of the city. During the Venice Biennale, the gardens serve as an outdoor exhibition space for contemporary art and architecture, making it both a cultural venue and a quiet retreat for locals and visitors alike.

shop_address: Riva dei Sette Martiri, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Went there to visit Bienale Arte di Venezia.
There is also a caffeteria where you can eat a delicious sandwich and a great cup of Italian coffee while visiting the pavillions.
Pics are from 2024.

shop_address: Calle Giazzo, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: The bienalle for architecture is both mind blowing and overwhelming. The 2025 installation was very focused on climate change, global growth and usage of resources and how materials/technologies are used for global problem solving. It is important to go in with an open mind and time to process the information. Some countries and exhibits are better than others in terms of interpretation and information. For the year 2025, the beauty is in the details. The exhibition u Is not about architecture as many think of it, but rather about elements of architecture. And, if you are not comfortable with the English or Italian language, you will likely be lost and confused. It is wise to get the three-day pass and break up your time to allow the ability to process the density of information. There are many restaurants and cafes in the Giardini but in the main bienalle exhibit in the arsenale area there is only one large cafe and restaurant. Be sure to download area maps before you explore to get a good sense of place and understand the exhibits before you explore. The water taxis will bring you to the main stops for arsenale and giardini.

shop_address: Calle Giazzo, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Compared to the rest of Venice, I found Giardini to be incredibly peaceful. Unlike much of the city, it's lush with greenery—trees, flowers, and open spaces. There are plenty of benches, and I enjoyed watching birds and butterflies flutter around. I came for the Biennale, but it was the tranquility of Giardini that truly impressed me.

shop_address: Calle Giazzo, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Beautiful location for the biennial art and architecture Biennales. At the Giardini are most of the national pavilions. At the 2025 Architecture Biennale I was particularly impressed by the US and Scandinavian pavilions with the former focusing on the porch in US architecture with great examples like Ocean Grove, New Jersey and Chautauqua, New York. The Greek pavilion was impressive as well with a focus on the two Venetian shipyards on the island of Crete. The Israeli pavilion was closed for renovation as was the pavilion of Venezuela, designed by Carlo Scarpa (1906-1978) who also designed the original entrance pavilion to the Biennale.

shop_address: Calle Giazzo, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Amazing garden with many sculptures. Wonderful view, great for a walk during a warm day. Fountains, playground and toilets available in the park.

review_text:
Amid the bustling canals and throngs of visitors that define Venice, the Giardini della Biennale offers a breath of calm. This leafy enclave, tucked along the Bacino di San Marco, isn’t a destination for grand botanical wonders, but its quiet charm and historical roots make it a worthy detour for those seeking respite.

Originally marshland transformed by Napoleon into public gardens, the space now serves dual purposes: a peaceful retreat and a cultural hub. Its winding paths lead visitors past 30 pavilions, each representing a different nation and showcasing architectural flair from icons like Carlo Scarpa and Alvar Aalto. Though the structures truly come alive during the Venice Biennale Art Festival—when global creativity takes center stage—they retain a quiet magnetism year-round.

The gardens’ unpretentious beauty is amplified by quirky details: feral cats lounging in sunlit corners, the stoic gaze of Garibaldi’s statue at the entrance, and a serene coastal walk that skirts the water’s edge. These elements stitch together a patchwork of tranquility, a stark contrast to the nearby chaos of St. Mark’s Square.

Yet, for all its understated appeal, the Giardini della Biennale remains a “nice-to-have” rather than a must-see. If your Venice itinerary is packed, you won’t miss a life-changing experience by skipping it. But for those with moments to spare, it’s a gentle reminder that even in a city overrun by tourism, pockets of stillness—and a few curious cats—still endure.

review_text:
“Tiny green escape in the heart of Venice—surprisingly refreshing! 🌿”

Stumbled upon Giardini Reali just behind Piazza San Marco, and it instantly became my go-to oasis in the city. With shaded benches, pergola-covered walkways, and a lovely view of the lagoon, it’s the perfect spot to recharge between sightseeing bursts  .

Unexpected calm: Even on busy days, the garden’s tucked-away location makes it feel almost secret—perfect for a pause with a book or a snack  .

Lovely design touches: A restored 19th-century greenhouse, drawbridge to Piazza San Marco, and the longest wisteria pergola in Venice give it both charm and elegance  .

Free and easy access: Open to the public, and ideal for hanging out while you wait for the vaporetto at Giardinetti stop. No tickets, no rush  .

shop_address: P.za San Marco, 30124 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Tucked away just around the corner from the bustling St. Mark’s Square, the Gardens of the Venice Foundation (Giardini della Fondazione Venezia) are a true hidden gem—a quiet haven of greenery and tranquility in a city better known for its canals and narrow alleys. We stumbled upon this secret spot during our visit and ended up spending a wonderfully peaceful hour here, simply relaxing with an ice cream and enjoying the natural beauty.

The gardens have an interesting history, originally part of the private grounds of a noble Venetian palace. Today, they are one of the few green spaces in the historic center, offering a rare escape from the crowds. The well-maintained paths wind through a delightful mix of Mediterranean plants, shady trees, and blooming flowers, creating an atmosphere that feels worlds away from the nearby tourist hotspots.

One of the most enjoyable aspects was the sense of seclusion—even though we were only minutes from the heart of Venice, the gardens felt almost undiscovered. The benches and quiet corners make it an ideal spot to pause, unwind, and soak in the beauty of the surroundings.

For anyone looking for a moment of calm in the midst of Venice’s energy, this is the perfect place. Whether you’re escaping the midday heat, looking for a quiet place to read, or simply wanting to appreciate another side of the city, the Gardens of the Venice Foundation are well worth seeking out.

review_text: The Giardini Reali in Venice, also known as the Royal Gardens, offer a serene escape from the bustling streets of the city. These historic gardens, originally created for royalty, boast lush greenery, vibrant flowers, and tranquil pathways perfect for leisurely strolls. Visitors can admire the elegant statues and fountains scattered throughout the park, while enjoying panoramic views of the Venetian lagoon. Whether relaxing on a bench or exploring the hidden corners, the Giardini Reali provide a peaceful retreat and a glimpse into Venice's regal past.

review_text:
This square ended up being our favorite thing while visiting Venice. Don't ask why the kids loved it as much as they did, but it gave them something to do for hours while giving us a place to sit in shade and observe the locals.

The pigeon to seagull ratio was perfect, the water fountain gave the chance for the kids to cool themselves down, and there were more locals chilling at the square than tourists. It shouldn't have been as perfect of a place to spend a couple hours once or twice, but it just happened to be the perfect Venezian playground for our kids. Thanks pigeons and the occasional seagull. P.S, one of the locals told us that chasing the seagulls could be a bit dangerous for kids, and they may be right, but our kids did drive the fear of God into the heart of every flying apparatus on this square.

shop_address: Viale Trento, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: The gardens were created in 1807 by order of Napoleon Bonaparte and designed by Gianantonio Selva as a public green space for the citizens of Venice. Today, they serve both as a peaceful retreat and as a major cultural venue. Since 1895, the gardens have hosted the Venice Biennale, beginning with its first International Art Exhibition. During the Biennale, approximately 65% of the gardens are dedicated to exhibition space, while the remaining 35% remains open as public parkland. Starting in 1907, individual nations began constructing their own pavilions within the gardens. Today, there are 29 national pavilions, making the gardens one of the central hubs for contemporary art during the Biennale.

review_text:
Surprising oasis of peace in a hectic city.  Come here, sit, walk your dog and enjoy the quiet.
Parco di Villa Groggia is a serene, lesser-known park in Venice's Cannaregio district, offering a peaceful retreat from the city's bustle. Visitors can enjoy shaded paths, benches, and the charming Teatrino Groggia, a small 19th-century theater hosting community events .

Historically, the park occupies the site of the 16th-century Palazzo Donà, demolished in 1823. In the late 19th century, Giuseppe Groggia developed the area into a romantic-style garden. After his death, the property was bequeathed to the San Gioacchino Institute in Mestre for the education of underprivileged girls. Since 1975, the City of Venice has owned and maintained the park .

Today, Parco di Villa Groggia serves as a tranquil space for locals and visitors alike, featuring shaded areas, benches, and a community center, making it an ideal spot to relax and experience a quieter side of Venice.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: I’ve recently came across this spectacular cafe in Venice. It sure has a very different vibe and atmosphere from all the other bars in Venice. It’s located in a small beautiful garden and they also sell plants. The plants, high roof, and the glass windows make it a very pretty place for relaxing and enjoying your day. We tried the hot chocolate with cream which I really liked, and the sweets (cupcake and small cornetto) tasted good too. The prices are about 1€ higher that other bars in Venice.

Stuff don’t speak English.
No reservations needed.

Overrated place, with high prices. Photogenic architecture and garden if you’re into Instagram places.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text:
Visited this place on the 19th of June 2024. Decent prices, decent drinks. I've visited it twice on the same day, at lunchtime, trying to find some shade, and once later the afternoon while my spose was visiting the Giardini della Bienalle . The shade was not the best, I can't really say that umbrellas provide the best shade, but the drinks were awesome (had some americano coffees, a machiato, some sprits) . They also had a power outlet inside so i charged my device while sipping my drinks.

Plus points for the venue, a former greenhouse dating back to 1890s.
Minus points for not having AC and maybe the waiters were a bit slow but spoke English and that was great.

I will add screenshots with their menu and peices as of June 2024 downloaded with their QR.

My first time experience there was super ok. Perfetto, like the italians would say 😁

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Situated amongst a busy plaza square, this is a beautiful restaurant with good food. We ate here before continuing our walk to the jazz club for a show. We were one of the first ones to arrive at dinner opening and got a table outside on the patio right away. As time went on, the restaurant became very busy and every table was taken. It became clear that the server was a bit overwhelmed as he was the only one attending to all tables outside. Nonetheless, still a great experience and it’s always a good rule of thumb to not be rushed when dining in Italy.
For the food, my partner ordered scallops and I ordered bruschetta. The scallops were not the best we’ve ever had but still good. My biggest con was that the olive oil was served in a reusable oil container and the cleanliness of the container was questionable. There were discolored spots within the container/oil. I had never seen olive oil in Italy served outside of its standard bottle.
We then ordered the lasagna to share as an entree. Now, the lasagna was some of the best I have ever had. It had an intense sweet note to it which was different and neat. My partner ordered some chocolate with an inner cookie type filling dessert and that did not last longer than 4 mins on the plate. Overall, a good experience and is a lovely place to eat outside on the patio and people watch with good food.

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
I’m a frequent traveler and restaurant-goer, but I rarely leave reviews—unless I’m truly impressed. And this restaurant completely blew me away. Everything was perfect: the location, the quality of the food, and the service!

I came two days in a row, and both times the experience was fantastic. I’m sharing photos of the three courses I ordered—because pictures speak louder than words. Each course was a masterpiece. Compliments to the Chef!

The atmosphere on Campo Santa Margherita is another great bonus—it's one of the most spacious squares in Venice and not overrun by tourist crowds.

Finally, I want to highlight Sali, our waiter, who is a true artist at what he does! I later found out that this is a family-run restaurant and that he’s actually a co-owner. That explained why I felt so welcome—the incredible quality here is clearly driven by love and dedication.

All in all, based on my many dining experiences around the world, this is easily one of the best restaurants I’ve visited in my 57 years. Highly recommended!

review_text:
This was a beautiful experience. There is a small tour which gives you enough information to inform and make the experience meaningful, but the tour guide allows you to experience the walk and music with yourself. The music composed specifically for the walk through the labyrinth is gorgeous, and walking quietly through while listening is a meditative experience, even if you are not a Borges readers. It was lightly raining, with sun, while we were there which added to the beauty!

We did this as a paired tour with the Vatican Chapels, and together they were an excellent experience.

We would love to experience it in another season, and will probably go back! Thank you!

Know before you go:
-You must book a ticket in advance through the Cini Foundation website.
-This is a symbolic labyrinth, not meant to be like a tourist corn maze or the like, more akin to a meditation garden.
-You should bring your own headphones if you can.

review_text:
出國那麼多地方，從來沒有住過那麼糟糕的房間（我是住單人房）。
第一，不僅要過好好幾關像廢墟的通道，才能進到房間，有夠麻煩，中途還有假人嚇你。
其次，一進房間讓我全身起雞皮疙瘩和不舒服。房間小就算了，有股難聞的味道，噴很多香水都無法蓋過，整個感到噁心，完全不想多待。
第三，房間已經夠小了，還放很大台又吵的空調，但不開又超悶，崩潰。
第四，總共四個房間卻要共要一個衛浴，實在不合理。
第一次不舒服到直接花錢訂另外一間，不然真的是花錢受罪。
最後，可以理解威尼斯本島的物價會比較貴和房偏小。但這間網路照片與現實不符，感覺被詐騙，大家不要來這邊住。

review_text: We loved this property so much we extended our stay twice! The location is absolutely wonderful for a family, and/or anyone with pets. The large park and play area were a big hit for our youngest two boys, who spent several hours a day exploring at the park. The water bus just a few steps away was fantastic for getting around, but we also loved the walk along the water. It’s a great way to get in some exercise after eating all of the wonderful food Venice has to offer! Other things we loved about the property were the large, comfortable rooms with plenty of seating for everyone, the well-equipped kitchen, and a printer!! We do online schooling and I can’t even begin to tell you how wonderful it was to have a laser printer available for our use!! The WiFi was exceptional as well. with four kids doing schoolwork and two adults utilizing the WiFi for our digital office we never experienced drag or downtimes. Great stay!

review_text: I WOULD’VE said I’d stay here again. That is no longer true. ||My husband, myself, and our infant daughter stayed here three nights. The first day was great; they actually gave us a bigger room than booked so our daughter had room to move around. Gave us the wifi, asked if we wanted breakfast included, etc, standard hotel things. The AC was difficult to work and were exhausted so we just went without overnight. Our daughter was really hot and sweaty and fell asleep an hour later than usual once we put a cool washcloth on her. In the morning, we were finally able to figure out the AC and left the hotel to explore for the day, excited to come back to a comfortable room. ||When we got back to our room in the late afternoon, the AC had been shut off and it was hotter in our room than outside. It was annoying but whatever so we turned it back on. We got some snacks for the night as I’m breastfeeding and get hungry in the middle of the night and didn’t think about it again. ||Until the third day. Our daughter woke up super early and they have quiet hours so we slipped out, got some pastries, and slipped back in to our room to not disturb anyone. There was also a sign with an X through it on pictures of food on the front door, so I was trying to be cautious that it seemed they didn’t want food in the foyer, which had some nice furniture in it. ||We went out for the day and when we came back in the afternoon, our daughter was screaming crying and overly tired, so we were hurrying back. When we got into the lobby, we were stopped by a new worker who told us that we were not allowed to have food in our room. I was confused why they were telling us this when we had openly brought up pastries and gelato a night before to our room and had not been told this rule. They said they found crumbs of our pastries and that food was not allowed but could be eaten in the common area which was behind the foyer. I was flustered by this so just said okay, explained about our tired baby, and took our daughter upstairs for her nap. ||My husband put our key in the lock and turned it both ways but it wouldn’t budge. He went downstairs and explained and the same lady came up with a master key and unlocked it. A special lock had been put in place that couldn’t be opened by our key, only hers. We took that to mean they did that so we would have to come down and be told about the food rule. They quite literally locked us out of our own hotel room that we had hurried back to for our screaming daughter just to make sure they told us to not eat food in the room. And the cherry on top, we got into the room, and the AC was off, AGAIN, leaving the room hot and it took longer for our daughter to fall asleep, needing a wet washcloth again. ||We were not told the rule about the food when we first came in nor we were told not to use the AC. The AC thing just seems like a  way to save money but it made us miserable especially when we were sweating from outside. ||The food rule also just seems dumb. We were only on the first floor, but this rule seemed excessive for someone on a much higher floor. Our hotel room is also the only spot our daughter could move around freely, so the idea of leaving it to go downstairs and eat a quick snack knowing she would be squirming around was not an easy concept to come to. Furthermore, there’s a tea kettle and a fridge in the room. If food isn’t allowed in the room, why the fridge? And outside of the one sticker on the door leading into the hotel, there was no other notice about the rule, which meant it was wildly up for interpretation. I took it to mean no food in the lobby because I’ve never been to a hotel that forbid food in the room. A better system would have been signs in the room, but there were none. ||This seems to me like a new rule. I looked at previous reviews and it seems they had an unresolved ant problem from September 2025, just a month earlier, so I’m guessing this is their bandaged way to fix the issue without getting rid of the ants. ||The location is great. But locking a guest out of their room, turning their AC off several times, and telling someone they can’t eat in their room isn’t telling of a hotel, but made me feel like I was in a dormitory.

Smells divine!||The wonderful owner Mara seems to know everyone and everything in Venice - she organised some very special private tours for us. |The staff - especially Ripon, Filippo and Albeto - and warm and helpful - nothing is too much trouble. ||Breakfast is a delicious extravaganza - in the garden, overlooking the canal - a great way to start the day. ||Our new home away from home in Venice.

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: A truly wonderful experience, we arrived by private transfer. This is the most expensive option but an experience that cannot be put in words as you arrive via the grand canal to the private garden entrance. The view from our window was to die for, seeing the city come to life each day was truly magical.||Madame Garden is not really a hotel, don’t expect generic service and breakfast buffet. This is a Venetian high end guest house. The staff are wonderful and genuine and you become part of the family. Recommendations are sent to you like it’s from a friend. The breakfast is served from the trolleys  brought to your table. Fruits , eggs and delicious pastries each day.||This is probably the best hotel I have stayed in , true memories.||A big thanks to all the team from Helen and Matthew

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Each of the reviews I read are spot-on: this is a fantastic place to stay! The location is absolutely perfect: about a 15-minute walk to the Rialto Bridge, and about 25-minutes to St. Mark's Square. It is so quiet, yet just minutes away from shopping and restaurants. The garden will thrill you! It so beautiful and peaceful. Breakfast (which is amazing) on the patio will get your day started the right way. We had the Iris canal/garden suite. It was so big and comfortable. Plus, we were right next to the canal and spent a lot of time watching the traffic on the canal. The staff, Mara, Alberto, Rippon and Lorenzo were fabulous. They will take care of your every need! We spent four nights there and it was perfect. I would not stay anywhere else in Venice. Thank you for taking such good care of us!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: This is my second stay at Madama. It is perfection. The decor is beautiful, rooms spacious and well set out, a gorgeous garden to chill in and watch the bustle on the canal. Breakfasts are delicious and extensive, you won't need lunch. What really adds to all this splendour is the staff, everyone is so friendly and helpful, lots of great recommendations on restaurants and places to go, you feel really looked after. Can't wait to go back.

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Beautiful and idyllic boutique hotel in a quiet location which is also within easy reach of attractions. Lovely Mara, the owner and her team are very attentive. Nothing is too much trouble. The breakfast is amazing as are the apartments, especially the comfy beds!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: What a wonderful personal oasis in Venice! The suite was amazing, with incredible views, and the service was above and beyond. Well worth it! Exactly the kind of romantic boutique hotel we were looking for. Highly recommended!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
My wife and I spent two nights in Madame Garden Retreat and it has been an unforgettable experience! On arrival, Alberto welcomed us and confirmed that we have been upgraded for our stay, which was a great start. Our room was clean, spacious and had great views over the canals. The property itself is tucked away from
the main tourist route, allowing you to enjoy a moment of quiet (and shade) in their garden over a glass of Aperol. During our stay, Alberto gave us some great tips that helped us take the most out of the visit. Overall, it felt like staying in someone’s Venetian house rather than a hotel, which made our time more special. We will most certainly be back!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Simply a wonderful tranquil garden spot to hide from the August crowds and to saunter the canals. Beautiful breakfast (the scrambled egg was just so yummy and there are more pastries, fruit and yoghurt than you could possibly wish for. Comfy bed, pretty decor, and staff who are very happy to make suggestions, seek reservations and to make your life easy. A fabulous hotel.

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: A timeless favorite in Venice and one of the most enjoyable museum experiences in the city. Entry was €16 per person, and absolutely worth it. The collection brings together some of the most iconic works of modern art — Pollock, Miró, Magritte, Calder, and of course, Peggy’s own spirit running through the house. The museum is compact but deeply personal, with pieces displayed in what was once her home overlooking the Grand Canal. We started with coffee and a slice of chocolate cake at the café (a bit ordinary, but the view made up for it) before exploring the galleries. Intimate, vibrant, and full of history — it’s easy to see why this is a favorite spot for so many visitors.

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
A must see when in Venice, totally worth the visit. I also recommend spending the extra €7 (on the steep side, to be fair) for the audioguide since there's an abundance of information regarding the artists and the collection in general (and the collectors themselves, of course). While a small gallery, the switch from garden to rooms to terrace (with exhibits) gives a nice and sophisticated feeling itself. You'll find the chance to see original Kandinsky, Picasso, Dali and Ernest (and many other famous contemporaries). I hope this helps 😊
P.S. while we booked in advance for early morning, there was no difficulty to also purchase tickets there. However, there was much more visitors than we expected, especially this early (first hours of opening).

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
WORLD CLASS! 👍👍👍👍👍
Here you will find a lot of the best artists of the best, a top place to visit in Venice. 🥰
They have a nice café. ☕🍷🍺
Entrance 15€, discount of you are owner 70 years old. 🙏
Picasso, Miro, Kandinsky and more of those guys.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
An absolute must when in Venice. The collection is incredible – not overwhelming in size, but perfectly curated so you can really enjoy each piece without feeling rushed. Walking through Peggy Guggenheim’s former home adds such a personal touch, and it feels almost like being invited into her world.

The sculpture garden is a highlight – peaceful, inspiring, and a lovely spot to pause before heading back inside. I loved how accessible the museum feels, even if you’re not an art expert, you can still connect with the works and the stories behind them.

It’s one of those places that stays with you long after you leave.

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Beautiful historic complex by the Canale Grande and quite an impressive collection. They also have temporary exhibitions of good quality. Be mindful though that accessibility is only warranted in the sculpture garden. Everywhere else there’s stairs up and down into and in the buildings.

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Probably the most visited museum for modern art in Venice. To avoid waiting for an hour in front of the ticket office, buy your ticket on the internet. The art is safe, e.g. Mrs Guggenheim preferred to invest in known artists rather than taking risks. Much of the pleasure of coming here is negatively affected by the affluence of visitors. Plan your visit accordingly if you can.

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
I enjoyed Peggy Guggenheim.  I am also a fan of contemporary/ Modern Art!  I am fascinated at how creative and ahead of their times the artists that she collected were in the 1930's.   Get the audio version, the back story helps you understand the relationships that Peggy had (or did not have) with the artists.
If you don't like contemporary art, go for the view it is beautiful overlooking the canal or in the garden.  Venezia!!! Venice is eternity itself.❤️

shop_address: Dorsoduro, 701, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Nice collection of modern artwork from Cubism, surrealism and abstract art and sculptures.  Works of Salvador Dali, Picasso and Joan Moro among others are displayed. Location along the Grand Canal is an ideal location. We spend quite a lot of time there. A nice cafe offers refreshing drinks too.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: What a great B&B this is!||||The apartment is located in a peaceful area of Venice great for exploring with good restaurants nearby. It’s only a 7 minutes walk to Piazza San Marco and a supermarket, pharmacy and delicious bakery can be found in close proximity. The location is excellent in Venice if you want to experience a local feel of the city away from other tourists and crowds. We loved it. The host Roberto was very welcoming and did everything to make sure we enjoyed our stay. He speaks very good English so any questions he is able to answer. The apartment was clean and spacious and included everything we needed. Highly recommended. ||||Giuseppe and Carline

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: My husband and I stayed for four nights in Roberto and Marina's apartment. It was in a fantastic location just off via Garibaldi with lots of lovely restaurants, market stalls and bars nearby. It was very easy to arrange with Roberto and Marina and Roberto showed us around and how everything worked. The price was very reasonable and they even include croissants, yoghurt, juice, tea, coffee, jam etc for breakfast. They checked on us by email to make sure we had everything we needed and were perfect hosts. I would definitely stay there again and highly recommend it to couples looking for space and facilities in a lovely area which is very close to the main tourist areas but has a local feel.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I can’t recommend Roberto and Marinas apartment enough! Roberto couldn’t have been more welcoming and lovely when we checked in, he met us outside his home and walked us to the apartment, showed us around and gave us recommendations for dinner! He made us feel very at ease and welcome! The apartment was lovely and well furnished with everything you could need for a short stay (we stayed 4 nights). It was in a perfect location, with shops and restaurants within a five minute walk and the main attractions within a 15 minute walk. Lovely peaceful area, away from all of the hustle and bustle. We hope to stay here again soon! Thank you Roberto and Marina :)

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I stayed here with my sister-n-law and niece for 3 nights (April 12th-14th) to start our trip and we just loved it. Roberto was the sweetest and prompt when corresponding via email prior to the trip.  He also gave us some great recommendations for dinner near the Apartment as well. They were great too!!||||The Apartment had more then enough space for the three of us and was equipped with the needed essentials for an enjoyable stay. ||||We would definitely stay here again when we visit next time.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I cannot recommend this B&B enough! I was only in contact with and met with Roberto on my short stay. Leading up to my trip I was provided with many useful and helpful directions on how to get to the location. Roberto also provided me with a phone number to contact if I got stuck or had any other questions to which he responded to very quickly.||The location where the B&B is situated is perfect! It's so peaceful and quiet enough to be away from all the busy areas but also has so much going on around it. There are many lovely restaurants, bars and a beautiful park nearby with very convenient water taxi stops close too. St Mark's square is also within a short 10 minutes walking distance too.||Roberto showed us exactly how everything worked within the apartment from the TV to the Air conditioning to the kitchen appliances and coffee maker. He also left the fridge full with refreshments and continental breakfast items. He couldn't do enough for us.||Check out was at 10am and our flight was not until night time but Roberto was more than happy to store away our luggage for us until we were ready to leave.||Highly recommend this B&B. Roberto was an amazing host and if I ever return to Venice I would 100% stay here again. Amazing value for money.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I loved the location of B & B Giardini.  You walk straight from piazza San Marcos to find this cozy and private apartment just up one flight of stairs in a very typical neighborhood.  The apartment is comfortable, with cooking facilities and there is a supermarket about a block away.  The Giardinis are around the corner if you like to take a walk, and there are plenty of nice restaurants in the area as well where you feel quite safe while eating al fresco.  Vaporetto 5.1 has a stop around the corner in case you bring your suitcases straight from the train station.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: The apartment was separate to the owners home and had a small kitchen, tiny bathroom And a small lounge and larger bedroom with a terrace full of old stuff but enough space to watch the people passing thru the street. ||At first we were disappointed by the standard but it was immaculately clean but very old and the furniture very mis matched. It is also up 4 flights of stairs and as my husband had just recovered from a heart attack not ideal. We did grow to love the place and the owner Marina was very nice and touched base with us to ensure all was OK. The location was excellent but we would not recommend it.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: We stayed here for 2 nights, it was a very convenient location for visiting the Biennale !  It is close to both Giardino and Arsenale. (walking distance) It is also not on the crowded touristy street in Venice, so if you are looking for a place that is quiet, this is a great choice ! The owner respond our email fast and great communication! Overall it was a great experience staying there !

review_text:
We had a fantastic stay at Campiello Zen! The place has been recently renovated with incredible thought and care, maintaining its historic charm. It’s spacious, comfortable, and ideally located for exploring Venice. It's a few minutes from Riva de Biasio station, so getting around with public transit was easy. The host’s restaurant recommendations were spot-on; we tried them all and loved them. Highly recommended!

Lastly, your stay might be joined by the friendliest turtle I've ever met!

shop_address: Santa Croce, 1285/piano terra, 30135 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: We had an absolutely amazing weekend, the apartment is perfectly located and beautifully furnished — a great mix of modern comfort and authentic charm. Everything we needed was there. The garden is simply magical, and we totally fell in love with the little turtle who stole our hearts instantly. Andrea was incredibly helpful with fantastic recommendations for places to visit and local restaurants. It truly was a fantastic weekend — we’re so grateful and would warmly recommend this apartment to anyone, with all our heart!

shop_address: Santa Croce, 1285/piano terra, 30135 Venezia VE, Italy

Abbiamo soggiornato nel nuovo appartamento Campiello Zen Garden e ci siamo trovati benissimo. C'è perfino un giardinetto esclusivo.
La posizione non potrebbe essere migliore: tranquillissima, fuori dalle zone turistiche più battute ma allo stesso tempo al centro di tutto. Difficile trovare soluzioni migliori a Venezia!

Atmosphere is lovey.

Food is fresh and delicious and what you would expect with reasonable pricing.
It’s a must place to expertise especially the terrace.

shop_address: Campo di Ghetto Nuovo, 30100 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
The food is really good -- definitely recommend if you want kosher food that seems to actually serve the local cuisine. The service is moderately good and was fine for English speakers.

Just beware, if you plan to use Google Maps to find this place, IT WILL NOT WORK. At the time of posting this, Google knew the actual location of the restaurant, the walking directions just led to a completely different location. You will need to find your own place.

Also, if you don't want to wait for a check, you can pay up front at the register.

shop_address: Campo di Ghetto Nuovo, 30100 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Situated in the heart of the Jewish Ghetto in Venice (right across from the museum) is this beautiful little kosher dairy/vegetarian restaurant with a wide selection of Italian dishes including pizza, salad, pasta, and fish. The seating is in a nice garden with some tables that even have a nice view of the adjacent canal. The food was decent, but the service was a bit confused.

Would have given 4 stars but they put out this bread on the table that was clearly just plain white store bread that wasn't even tasty. Why not get some nice bread for your customers? There's even a kosher bakery a minute away that sold nice looking breads.

review_text: The food was nice and the service was ok. This place is 100% kosher. Outdoor setting is nice, but be careful of mosquitoes.

review_text: This place is hands down better than the kosher meat option in Venice. The pizza was out of this world, fish was incredible, as well as the dessert! We loved the decor and ambience here. The restaurant is on the canal, in case you’re willing to face the cold and eat outside. Highly recommend!!! Service was also great, even when busy!!

review_text:

На місці можна взяти в оренду парасольку, є туалет, душова та перевдягальня, проте за кожне потрібно доплачувати окремо. Лежаків, на жаль, немає 😅 трохи було ніяково, коли ми знайшли місце щоб сісти на пісочку (ми не займали лежанок чи тощо)  а прийшов робітник з сусіднього пляжу і почав нас виганяти бо наче б то ми перетнули межу безкоштовного і їхнього, нам довелося переносити речі буквально на 2 метри 🤦🏻‍♀️
В цілому відпочинок на пляжі нам сподобався. Море надзвичайно гарне 🌊

review_text: Il servizio è molto buono ma poco flessibile. Ho dovuto ritirare i bagagli letteralmente 15 minuti dopo il deposito a causa di un imprevisto, ho contattato tempestivamente i gestori ma non mi hanno offerto nessun aiuto. Quindi attenzione perché una volta che il servizio è pagato (minimo 24h) e si dovesse cambiare idea non si riceverà assistenza, neanche in caso si riutilizzasse lo stesso servizio il giorno successivo. Colpa mia ovviamente, ma da un servizio con un numero di assistenza dedicato penso ci si possa aspettare qualcosa di più.
